_comment: AUTHORITATIVE tracker beacon contract. Each entry is the exact, sorted set of property names the tracker JS emits for that event_type after the full send pipeline (manageState -> globals -> defaults). This file is the single source of truth shared by the JS and PHP test layers: tests/js/BeaconContract.test.js asserts the tracker still emits exactly these keys (catches tracker-side renames/drops), and the PHP ingestion tests assert every handler-consumed field is present here (catches server-side drift). Regenerate with tests/js/BeaconContract.test.js when the tracker legitimately changes, and review the matching handler when a name changes. Note: is_new_session / is_new_visitor appear only on the first beacon of a new session/visitor; a session-update beacon (active session) omits is_new_session. The 'base.page_request' entry is a direct (no-referrer, no-campaign) new-session pageview. 'base.page_request.referral' and 'base.page_request.campaign' are the two mutually-exclusive referred new-session shapes the tracker emits: a referrer-inferred visit carries session_referer (and the server derives source/medium/referer from it), while a campaign-tagged visit (owa_* URL params) carries campaign/source/medium/search_terms/attribs INSTEAD of session_referer (the campaign attribution path suppresses referrer inference). These are captured by tests/js/BeaconContractReferred.test.js.
